verses.text: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.
And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.

blurb: In a culture that constantly tells us we need more—more success, more recognition, more stuff—Paul's words cut through the noise with startling clarity. True wealth isn't found in accumulation but in the quiet satisfaction of having what we need. On this ordinary Saturday, these verses remind us that contentment is not settling for less, but recognizing that godliness paired with gratitude for simple provisions is the richest life of all.
